name: Orange Kiss-me Cake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Merrill. This recipe was the 1950 $25,000 winner in the Pillsbury bakeoff. Use Pillsbury's Best all-purpose flour or unbleached flour; do not use self-rising flour. Preheat oven to 350°F (325°F if using a glass pan). Grease and flour a 13 x 9 baking pan.
1. Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off.
2. Squeeze orange and reserve 1/3 cup juice. Grind the peel, pulp, raisins, and walnuts together; set aside.
3. In a large bowl, blend flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt with milk and shortening using medium speed until just combined.
4. Add eggs and beat for 3 minutes.
5. Stir in the orange-raisin mixture.
6. Pour batter into prepared pan.
7. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Topping: Drizzle reserved orange juice over warm cake. Combine sugar, cinnamon, and walnuts; sprinkle over the cake. Garnish with orange slices if desired.
High altitudes: Above 3,500 feet, add 2 tablespoons flour; bake at 375°F for 35-40 minutes.
